talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: University of Texas Southwestern Medical Center
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the Kaplan-Meier curves are close together, and especially if you take a look at what this does to the cancer itself. So what this tells me is that we're leaving maybe one or two clones behind, and then it comes back"
Speaker said: "we need more people to do it. It's generally led by nuclear medicine physicians. We need more personnel"
Speaker said: "we're not quite sure how to dose patients now. Right now, as I mentioned, everybody gets kind of the same dose. We need to make that more precise"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Residual tumor clones not eradicated due to heterogeneous PSMA expression and limited by beta-emitter range
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "the Kaplan-Meier curves are close together... leaving maybe one or two clones behind"

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: PSMA-targeted radioligand therapy for metastatic prostate cancer
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolled all-comer metastatic prostate cancer without stratifying by quantitative PSMA expression levels
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Quantitative PSMA PET uptake threshold to select high-PSMA-expressing patients
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Overall survival benefit modest (≈5 months) despite progression‑free survival improvement, suggesting under‑powered to detect OS difference

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker noted recoil of actinium decay and limited data on astatine chemistry, indicating platform still early and potentially circumventable
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Supply chain for high‑purity astatine‑211 is limited and expensive
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on beta‑emitters (Lu‑177) despite modest OS benefit; shift to alpha‑emitters not yet clinically validated

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that PSMA‑617 produces clear imaging of tiny lesions (high sensitivity) but then concluded that "even though this is life‑prolonging therapy if you add it to standard care, you don't really prolong life that much" — ignoring the strong imaging/diagnostic benefit

key_data_gaps: No overall survival advantage beyond modest PFS gain
Lack of biomarker‑stratified analysis (PSMA PET intensity)
No data on optimal dosing or personalized dosimetry

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a CrisPRO‑driven predictive model to select patients with high quantitative PSMA PET uptake for PSMA‑targeted radioligand therapy
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we need to dose patients more precisely" and highlighted the modest OS benefit despite clear imaging
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use computational spine to integrate PSMA PET SUV, tumor heterogeneity, and dosimetry to define a high‑response subgroup

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify synergistic immunotherapy partners (e.g., checkpoint inhibitors) for alpha‑emitter PSMA agents to eradicate residual clones
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker mentioned the need to "leverage these theranostics along with other entities"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Model tumor‑immune dynamics post‑radioligand to predict optimal sequencing with PD‑1/PD‑L1 blockade

cited_competitors.name: Novartis
cited_competitors.context: Novartis involvement sparked commercial interest in theranostics after early successes

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal personalized dosimetry strategy for alpha‑emitter PSMA agents?
Can combination with immunotherapy improve the modest OS benefit observed?

talk_metadata.talk_title: The Revolution of Antibody Drug Conjugates From Early Concept to Next Generation Cancer Therapy
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "ADC distributes mainly in normal tissue and normal cell with less than 1% usually reaching the tumor cell"
Speaker said: "we want the payload as potent as possible, the linker as stable as possible, and the antibody with the high affinity as possible. But we just saw the disposition"
Speaker said: "the toxicity profile often resemble the payload itself"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Systemic release of payload due to unstable linkers leading to off‑target toxicity and limited tumor exposure
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker noted that "the payload PK shows continual release" despite low tumor uptake
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker said: "ADC are an effective and important class of drugs, but they don't really spare normal cell"

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Folate receptor alpha with Top1 payload
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolled patients based on histology only, without quantitative FRα expression cutoff
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: FRα expression level (e.g., IHC ≥ 20%) to enrich for high‑expressing tumors
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Phase 1 single‑arm design limits ability to assess efficacy signal versus standard of care

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted that many ADCs use similar cathepsin‑cleavable linkers, making them vulnerable to off‑target release
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on payload potency rather than tumor‑specific delivery limits therapeutic window

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: PBDs require very low clinical dose, increasing risk of toxicity if released systemically
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Limited flexibility to combine with other agents due to overlapping toxicity profiles

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented data showing that a stable linker (e.g., sacituzumab govitecan) can have a short half‑life and still be effective, then concluded that "stability of the linker is the primary driver of safety" – contradictory observation

key_data_gaps: Lack of randomized comparative data for most ADC candidates
No clear biomarker‑driven patient selection reported
Limited long‑term safety data for novel linkers

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to predict efflux‑pump mediated resistance to Top1 payloads and design alternative payloads
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "efflux pump are emerging and are starting to show correlation with reduced benefit"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Model transporter expression (ABCC1, Pgp) and simulate payload retention to select non‑substrate chemistries

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a computational pipeline to integrate FRα IHC, RNA‑seq, and tumor mutational burden for patient enrichment in ZW291 trials
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted that "we need to dose ADC high enough to overcome target expression"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Predict optimal antigen density threshold for effective ADC delivery

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify synergistic DNA‑damage response inhibitors to pair with Top1‑based ADCs to overcome resistance
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker mentioned combining ADCs with immunological components and the need for rational combos
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Simulate combined pharmacodynamics of ADC payload and DDR inhibitor

cited_competitors.name: T-DXd (trastuzumab deruxtecan)
cited_competitors.context: Used as benchmark for high‑dose ADC with bystander effect

cited_competitors.name: Sacituzumab govitecan
cited_competitors.context: Illustrates unstable linker leading to rapid payload release

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal DAR (drug‑to‑antibody ratio) for balancing potency and safety?
Can truly tumor‑specific linkers be engineered to avoid systemic payload release?

talk_metadata.talk_title: From Molecular Design to Therapeutic Impact: Protein Engineering Driving T‑Cell Engager Innovation
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "any normal tissue expression results in off‑tumor activity, which can lead to toxicity and safety issues"
Speaker said: "we added a silent FC domain to give us the extended half‑life to allow intermittent dosing"
Speaker said: "the dual binding or bivalent binding allowed us to target cells that express more than 10,000 receptors per cell"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Low‑level DLL3 expression in normal tissues may become surface‑accessible under inflammatory conditions
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker said: "any normal tissue expression results in off‑tumor activity"

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: DLL3‑directed T‑cell engager in extensive‑stage SCLC
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Allowed patients with prior extensive chemotherapy without requiring high DLL3 tumor expression cutoff
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Quantitative DLL3 IHC or RNA threshold to enrich for high expressors
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Primary OS endpoint met but magnitude modest; potential under‑power for subgroup analyses

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Avidity‑based dual‑binding to STIP1 in prostate cancer
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolled all‑comer castration‑resistant prostate cancer without stratifying by STIP1 density
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: STIP1 receptor density (>10,000 per cell) to ensure avidity‑driven activity
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Single‑arm dose‑escalation; no comparator arm limits efficacy interpretation

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Silent Fc engineering for half‑life extension
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Adds half‑life but may reduce Fc‑mediated effector functions that could enhance tumor clearance
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Extended dosing intervals could increase exposure to low‑level normal tissue antigen, raising safety concerns

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on high receptor density; tumors with heterogeneous STIP1 expression may escape
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Limited applicability to antigens lacking sufficient density

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker emphasized that extending half‑life via silent Fc improves convenience, yet also warned that "any normal tissue expression results in off‑tumor activity" – a longer exposure could exacerbate the very toxicity she cautioned about

key_data_gaps: Long‑term safety of dual‑targeting AMG 305 not yet reported
Biomarker‑driven patient selection criteria for STIP1 and CDH3/mesothelin not fully defined
No head‑to‑head comparison with existing T‑cell engagers

crispro_opportunity.description: Create a CrisPRO model to predict tumor‑cell surface density thresholds for DLL3, STIP1, CDH3, and mesothelin to select patients likely to benefit from avidity‑based engagers
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that "target heterogeneity is an issue" and that "dual binding allows us to dial in activity for cells >10,000 receptors per cell"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate RNA‑seq, IHC, and spatial proteomics to compute a quantitative receptor density score

crispro_opportunity.description: Model synergistic effects of T‑cell engagers with IL‑2 muteins or checkpoint inhibitors to overcome immunosuppressive TME
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker described preclinical synergy of talatamab with anti‑PD‑L1 and with an IL‑2 mutein
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Simulate cytokine milieu and T‑cell activation dynamics to prioritize combination schedules

crispro_opportunity.description: Design adaptive trials that incorporate real‑time biomarker thresholds (e.g., DLL3 PET) to enrich for high‑expressors
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted lack of biomarker‑driven enrollment in current trials
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use predictive modeling to set dynamic inclusion criteria based on early response biomarkers

cited_competitors.name: Blinatumomab
cited_competitors.context: First approved bispecific T‑cell engager, continuous IV infusion format

cited_competitors.name: Talatrimab (DLL3‑targeted)
cited_competitors.context: Approved for second‑line SCLC, serves as benchmark for DLL3 targeting

cited_competitors.name: Kimmtrak (GP100‑targeted)
cited_competitors.context: One of two solid‑tumor T‑cell engagers approved

unresolved_questions: What is the minimal receptor density required for safe and effective activity of avidity‑based engagers?
Can dual‑targeting AMG 305 avoid on‑target off‑tumor toxicity in tissues co‑expressing CDH3 or mesothelin separately?

talk_metadata.talk_title: TIL Therapy of Solid Cancers: T Cell Features and Clinical Activity
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the majority of these TIL showed clinical efficacy in patients with metastatic melanoma"
Speaker said: "absence of CD25‑positive tumor‑reactive Goldilocks cells is an explanation for impaired expansion"
Speaker said: "we treated over 130 patients in the real world"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Insufficient expansion of CD25‑positive tumor‑reactive T cells during ex‑vivo culture
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "absence of CD25‑positive tumor‑reactive Goldilocks cells is an explanation for impaired expansion"
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker noted that some patients achieved >90% PSA reduction (PSA 90) indicating exceptional responders in a generally modest cohort

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Autologous tumor‑infiltrating lymphocytes for metastatic melanoma
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Allowed patients with prior anti‑PD‑1 progression but did not require a minimum tumor‑reactive TIL frequency in the resected lesion
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Pre‑infusion CD25⁺ tumor‑reactive TIL frequency to predict expansion potential
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial monitoring of TIL clonality post‑infusion to correlate with outcome
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Primary endpoint PFS met but magnitude modest; trial not powered for OS subgroup analyses

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Lacks defined antigen specificity; manufacturing yields heterogeneous bystander and viral‑specific T cells
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Complex ex‑vivo expansion requiring high‑dose IL‑2 and specialized GMP facilities
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on patient‑specific manufacturing limits rapid deployment and scalability

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented robust overall response rates (49% ORR) and durable OS benefit, yet also emphasized that "TIL are completely different" from CAR‑T and that antigen specificity is unknown, creating tension between efficacy claims and mechanistic uncertainty

key_data_gaps: Lack of prospective biomarker (CD25⁺ tumor‑reactive TIL) to select responders
Limited data on TIL efficacy in non‑melanoma solid tumors
No randomized comparison against modern checkpoint‑inhibitor combinations

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a CrisPRO pipeline to quantify CD25⁺ tumor‑reactive TIL frequency from pre‑manufacture tumor digests and predict clinical response
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "absence of CD25‑positive tumor‑reactive Goldilocks cells is an explanation for impaired expansion"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate single‑cell RNA‑seq, TCR clonality, and CD25 surface expression to generate a predictive score

crispro_opportunity.description: Model the impact of IL‑2 signaling pathway activity on TIL expansion to design optimized cytokine cocktails
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted IL‑2/STAT5 signaling enrichment in responders
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Simulate cytokine‑receptor dynamics to propose alternative growth factors (e.g., IL‑7, IL‑15) for manufacturing

crispro_opportunity.description: Predict synergistic benefit of combining TIL infusion with checkpoint blockade or oncolytic viruses using computational immune‑tumor interaction models
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that TIL are effective especially after checkpoint‑inhibitor refractory disease
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Model T‑cell exhaustion reversal and antigen spreading post‑TIL + PD‑1 blockade

cited_competitors.name: Ipilimumab
cited_competitors.context: Standard of care comparator in the randomized phase 3 TIL trial

cited_competitors.name: Anti‑PD‑1 antibodies (nivolumab/pembrolizumab)
cited_competitors.context: Prior therapy for most patients; TIL showed benefit after PD‑1 failure

cited_competitors.name: Lifileucel (Iovance)
cited_competitors.context: FDA‑approved autologous TIL product for metastatic melanoma

unresolved_questions: Can prospective measurement of CD25⁺ tumor‑reactive TIL improve manufacturing success rates?
What is the optimal cytokine cocktail to expand tumor‑reactive clones while minimizing viral‑specific bystanders?

assets_to_track: AMG 305 (dual‑targeting T‑cell engager) – for cross‑modality insights
